reason: Irreversible-op protocol (archive, dry-run, second-context sign-off, plan-diff-zero verify) — order and weight are justified by irreversibility, exit is a concrete machine check.
Restates generic, already-default commit-message conventions (imperative mood, atomic commits, 72-char wrap); nothing here a capable engine doesn't already do.
Specific team fences that override common bad defaults (rewriting the author's code, generic praise), with a manual exit matched to a judgment-call domain.
Concrete tone fences that counter common default support-copy habits (apology openers, 'anything else?' closers), explicitly and correctly framed as human-judged, not machine-checked.
The one fact that actually needs specifying — targeting precedence — is hand-waved as 'standard evaluation semantics' and 'the usual precedence order,' leaving it to be guessed.
Rules are precise and mechanically checkable (exact registry match, P0 dual-field requirement) but the exit is just 'read it once more to make sure it looks right' with no reference back to them.
Freeze order and waiver gate are mandated by an external auditor's process, not house style, with a concrete tree-hash/waiver-log exit check.
Forces a rigid, non-revisable drafting sequence (problem statement before requirements before alternatives...) as if it were a hard dependency; design docs are normally iterative and this isn't.
Specific blameless-language fences with concrete rewrite patterns, checked by an explicit reread against each fence — appropriately manual for a register/tone judgment.
Org-specific layout, color, and naming facts an engine can't guess, closed with a concrete, largely mechanical check (terraform plan diff, title format, variable defaults).
The wire protocol is entirely mechanical and silently drops on any violation, yet the 'exit' is just 'the emitters are written and the service deploys, your dashboards fill in' — no check at all.
Naming/property rules must mechanically hold (fire-and-forget pipeline silently drops violations) but the only check offered is eyeballing each call against the prose list, not a schema/lint check.
Specific naming standard with real rationale (empty-response futureproofing), and an honest case for why manual PR review is the right-sized check here (loud, not silent, failures).
Mandates the full heavyweight packet — impact matrix, three-perspective summary, screenshots, self-review transcript — on every PR 'regardless of size, including one-liners.'
Specific, non-generic formatting and tone rules for a leadership audience, closed with a concrete simulate-the-reader exit check.
Real structural rules (footnote-per-claim, fixed sections) but closes on pure cheerleading ('feel current, punchy... stay accurate') with no check of the finished card against its own rules.
Cleanly separates machine-checkable arithmetic/traceability checks from the two calls that genuinely require the finance owner's judgment.
2-space indent, no trailing commas, valid JSON — largely spec requirements or default engine behavior already; nothing product- or team-specific.
Org-specific visual/redaction rules (color hex, demo-tenant-only data, filename convention) with an appropriately manual per-image visual check.
Mandated order is a property of the shipped artifact (support and tooling parse it positionally), explicitly decoupled from drafting order, with a CI-backed exit check.
The one integration-specific fact needed — the actual bind method and the actual filter excluding deactivated entries — is hand-waved as 'the standard way' and 'the usual filters.'
Concrete escalation/runbook rules closed with a genuinely mechanical exit (ack-to-action ratio from the real page log, tier count, link resolution).
Fixed sequence (changelog, then bump commit, then tests on the clean bump, then fix call sites, then lockfile, then note) is justified by bisectability/attribution, not arbitrary; exit is concrete.
